'''Keith Galloway''' (born 2 September 1985) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|pr}} in the 2000s and 2010s.

He played for the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] and the [[Wests Tigers]] in the [[NRL]], and the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[Super League]]. Galloway played for [[City New South Wales rugby league team|City Origin]], [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] in the [[State of Origin series]] and [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] at international level. He was known for his size and power.<ref>{{cite news|newspaper=The Sydney Morning Herald|title=Every magician needs a prop, and for every spellbinding Marshall there's a Galloway|author=Glenn Jackson|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/every-magician-needs-a-prop-and-for-every-spellbinding-marshalltheres-a-galloway-20091123-j0ob.html|access-date=30 October 2010|date=28 April 2009}}</ref>

==Background==
Galloway was born in [[Sydney]], New South Wales, Australia. He is of Scottish descent.

==Biography==
While attending [[Marist College Kogarah]], Galloway played for the NSW under 17s and 19s<ref>{{cite news|newspaper=The Sydney Morning Herald|title=Big red feels right at home with the Blues, thanks to some familiar faces|author=Josh Rakic|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/big-red-feels-right-at-home-with-the-blues-thanks-to-some-familiar-faces-20110701-1guwk.html|date=2 July 2011}}</ref> and then [[Australia national schoolboys rugby league team|Australian Schoolboys team]] in 2002.<ref>{{cite web|title=SportingPulse Homepage for Australian Secondary Schools Rugby League|url=http://www.sportingpulse.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=7-2130-0-0-0&sID=26424|access-date=2008-10-10|publisher=SportingPulse|archive-date=31 January 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170131030043/http://websites.sportstg.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=7-2130-0-0-0&sID=26424|url-status=dead}}</ref> Galloway played junior football with the Brighton Seagulls in the St George District, then Mascot Juniors in the South Sydney Juniors before being enticed to switch to the [[Yarrawarrah Tigers]] by mentor [[Robert Stone (rugby league)|Robert Stone]] as a teenager.<ref>{{cite news|newspaper=The Sydney Morning Herald|title=Galloway won't let sentiment get in way|author=Glenn Jackson|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-match-preview/galloway-wont-let-sentiment-get-in-way-20100919-15i13.html|access-date=20 September 2010|date=30 October 2010}}</ref>

===Playing career===
====Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks====
Galloway made his NRL début for the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] in 2003 while still attending high school, a few months before his 18th birthday. He made 8 appearances from the bench in the second half of the 2003 season, scoring a try in his second match.

Starting the 2004 season on the bench, Galloway scored the Sharks' sole try in the opening game of the season, a 6–12 loss to Wests Tigers. He played in 14 games for the season, including some mid-season appearances in the starting side in the second-row.

Galloway was on the receiving end of a [[John Hopoate]] leaping elbow charge in 2005, leaving the 19-year-old unconscious on the field. The event led to a 17-week suspension for Hopoate.<ref>{{cite news|publisher=ABC News|title=Hopoate sacked after 17-match suspension|url=http://www.abc.net.au/news/stories/2005/03/22/1329510.htm|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101122185738/http://www.abc.net.au/news/stories/2005/03/22/1329510.htm|url-status=dead|archive-date=22 November 2010|access-date=2008-09-05|date=2005-03-23}}</ref> He was again a regular on the bench for the Sharks, but in the qualifying final match against [[St. George Dragons]] he was promoted to starting prop, in what was his last game for the club.
[[File:Robbie Farah and Keith Galloway (26 April 2009).jpg|200px|thumb|left|Galloway playing for Wests in 2009]]
[[File:Keith Galloway (4439186888).jpg|200px|thumb|right|Galloway in 2010]]

====Wests Tigers====
=====2006–2011=====
Galloway joined the [[Wests Tigers]] in 2006, having signed with them halfway through the 2005 season. He said, "I'd played with a few of those boys with schoolboys and junior rep teams. It was a really young squad then. I thought it would probably be a good opportunity to grow with this team."<ref name=tk>{{cite web| work= Talking With TK | title=Episode 115 – Keith Galloway | url=https://www.talkingwithtk.com/single-post/Keith-Galloway}}</ref> He played 12 games in his first season, mostly off the bench, failing to score a try. In 2006, he spent more time as the starting prop and made 17 appearances. He scored his first try for the club in round 3 against Parramatta.

He was named in the Scotland training squad for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Henderson trio in Scots cup squad">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7587971.stm|title=Henderson trio in Scots cup squad|publisher=BBC|date=2008-08-29|access-date=2008-10-03}}</ref>

He was selected for City in the [[City vs Country Origin|City vs Country]] match on 8 May 2009.<ref>{{cite news|publisher=[[Fox Sports (Australia)|Fox Sports]]|location=Australia|title=Blues halfback duel in two as Mitchell Pearce misses selection|first=Todd|last=Balym|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,25422326-5016947,00.html|archive-url=https://archive.today/20121205020103/http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,25422326-5016947,00.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=2012-12-05|date=2009-05-03|access-date=2009-05-03}}</ref> Before the [[2009 State of Origin series]] Galloway was named in the 40 man [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] preliminary squad but was ultimately not available for selection due to injury.<ref>{{cite news|newspaper=The Sydney Morning Herald|title=Anasta and Mason left out of 40-man Origin squad|url=http://www.smh.com.au/articles/2009/04/21/1240079653254.html|access-date=2009-05-26| date=21 April 2009}}</ref> Later that year he was named in Australia's Four-Nations train-on squad.<ref>{{cite news
 |publisher=livenews.com.au 
 |title=Kangaroos selectors stick with tried and tested 
 |url=http://livenews.com.au/league/kangaroos-selectors-stick-with-tried-and-tested/2009/9/7/218650
 |access-date=10 September 2009 
 |date=7 September 2009 
}}{{dead link|date=May 2017 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}</ref> He was also picked for the Prime Minister's XIII to play Papua New Guinea,<ref>{{cite news|publisher=Fox Sports|location=Australia|title=Penrith's Petero Civoniceva named captain of Prime Minister's XIII|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,26071164-5018866,00.html|access-date=15 September 2009|date=14 September 2009|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090927062122/http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,26071164-5018866,00.html|archive-date=27 September 2009}}</ref> but had to withdraw due to an injury to his left knee.<ref>{{cite news|work=The Daily Telegraph|location=Australia|title=Matt Scott called into Prime Minister's XIII|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/matt-scott-called-into-prime-ministers-xiii/story-e6frexnr-1225778583001|access-date=10 October 2009|date=23 September 2009}}</ref>

In 2010, he signed a contract to remain at the club until the end of the 2013 season.<ref>{{cite news|newspaper=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|title=Queensland State of Origin selectors to keep eye on Lote Tuqiri's progress|author=Christian Nicolussi|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/queensland-state-of-origin-selectors-to-keep-eye-on-lote-tuqiris-progress/story-e6frexnr-1225841622932|access-date=27 March 2010|date=17 March 2010}}</ref>

In 2011, Galloway made his [[State of Origin series|State of Origin]] début, coming off the bench in game 3 of the series, but had little time on the field.<ref>{{cite news|publisher=The Roar|title=Origin 3: NSW Blues player ratings|author=Ryan O'Connell|url=http://www.theroar.com.au/2011/07/07/origin-3-nsw-player-ratings|access-date=6 October 2011|date=7 July 2011}}</ref> At the end of the season, Galloway became the first Wests Tigers' prop to be chosen in an Australian squad. He said of the selection, "I'm super stoked to be there. I was happy with my form this year, I didn't have many injuries and I got through 23 games, which is the most I've played. Consistency-wise it was my best year."<ref>{{cite news|newspaper=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|title=Keith Galloway ends Tiger prop curse|author=Christian Nicolussi|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/keith-galloway-ends-tiger-prop-curse/story-e6frexnr-1226157571596|access-date=6 October 2011|date=4 October 2011}}</ref>

Galloway made his test début against New Zealand, coming off the bench to score a try in the second half, in a game won easily by Australia. He later said, "I just ran off [[Billy Slater]]. I didn't have to run far for the try."<ref name=tk /> He went on to play in all four of Australia's [[2011 Rugby League Four Nations|Four Nations]] matches. He was awarded the [[Noel Kelly (rugby league)|Noel Kelly]] Medal for Best Forward at the Wests Tigers for the year.<ref name="ozzie">{{cite news|publisher=Ozzie News|title=Gareth Ellis is Wests Tigers Player of the Year|url=http://www.ozzienews.com/sport/gareth-ellis-is-wests-tigers-player-of-the-year|access-date=3 January 2012|date=17 October 2011}}</ref>

=====2012–15=====
Injuries hampered Galloway's chances of playing representative football in 2012.<ref>{{cite news|author=Greg Prichard and Michael Chammas|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|title=Pride and passion the key as Galloway awaits a call-up|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/pride-and-passion-the-key-as-galloway-awaits-a-callup-20120529-1zhgi.html|access-date=15 September 2012|date=30 May 2012}}</ref> He made 18 appearances for the Wests Tigers throughout the season, with the club losing every game that he was unable to play in.

A torn pectoral muscle in the round two game saw Galloway miss seventeen weeks of football in 2013, playing just 9 games. In July, he re-signed with the club for another three seasons. Coach [[Mick Potter]] said, "Keith consistently makes the most metres which gives the team that much needed go forward. He definitely has a presence on the field, being a big man he stops people on contact."<ref>{{cite news|publisher=ABC News|title=NRL: Wests Tigers re-sign Keith Galloway for three more seasons|url=http://www.abc.net.au/news/2013-06-07/galloway-re-signs-with-tigers/4741234|access-date=23 September 2013|date=7 June 2013}}</ref> Galloway was expected to play for [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] in the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup|2013 World Cup]], but withdrew with after sustaining a stress facture in his foot in Wests last game for the season.<ref>{{cite news|publisher=Sky Sports|title=Rugby League World Cup: Keith Galloway and James McManus withdraw from Scotland squad|url=http://www1.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12040/8935607/galloway-and-mcmanus-withdraw-from-scottish-world-cup-squad|access-date=23 September 2013|date=22 September 2013}}</ref>

Galloway played in 22 games in 2015, the most appearances he had made in a regular season. In round 7 against the Canberra Raiders, he scored his first try since the middle of 2012. He made 502 tackles for the year and averaged over a hundred metres per game with the ball. In round 25, with Wests Tigers well ahead on the scoreboard and regular time over, Galloway attempted his first conversion, which narrowly missed.<ref>{{cite web| work=weststigers.com.au| title=2015 Season Review: Keith Galloway| author=Alicia Newton and Dan Talintyre| url=http://www.weststigers.com.au/news/2015/09/24/_2015_season_review_.html| access-date=18 October 2015| archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151012063408/http://www.weststigers.com.au/news/2015/09/24/_2015_season_review_.html| archive-date=12 October 2015| url-status=dead}}</ref> Galloway said, "I didn't want to do it, but everyone in the team egged me on to do it. Obviously I'm not a goal-kicker. I sort of knew it was my last home game but it probably didn't hit me until we were out there. I've had a great 10 years – I really enjoyed it."<ref>{{cite news| work= The Sydney Morning Herald| title=End of an era as Pat Richards and Keith Galloway farewell Wests Tigers fans| author=Chris Barrett|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/wests-tigers/end-of-an-era-as-pat-richards-and-keith-galloway-farewell-wests-tigers-fans-20150830-gjb4mc.html|access-date=18 October 2015|date=30 August 2015}}</ref>

====Leeds Rhinos====
On 24 July 2015, Keith signed a three-year contract to play for the [[Super League]] heavyweights the [[Leeds Rhinos]] beginning in the 2016 season.<ref>{{cite news 
| title = Galloway signs three-year deal at Rhinos
| newspaper = The Yorkshire Post
| date = 2015-07-25
| url = http://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leeds-rhinos/leeds-rhinos-former-australian-test-forward-galloway-signs-three-year-deal-at-rhinos-1-7376157
| access-date = 2015-08-04}}</ref>

After travelling to the UK, Galloway said that he intended to represent Scotland in 2016 Four Nations, and would have liked to have played for them in the past if not for injuries. He said, "My father came over to Australia when he was in his 20s. I'm Australian but I'm proud of my Scottish ancestry and it would mean a lot to my family and myself if I could pull on that jersey."<ref>{{cite news| work= The Sydney Morning Herald| title=Former Australian prop Keith Galloway set to play for Scotland Bravehearts| url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/former-australian-prop-keith-galloway-set-to-play-for-scotland-bravehearts-20160126-gmdziu.html|access-date=26 January 2016|date=30 January 2016}}</ref>

== Career highlights ==
*''First Grade Debut:'' 2003 – Round 19, Cronulla v [[New Zealand Warriors]] at [[Toyota Park (Cronulla)|Toyota Park]], [[Sydney]], 19 July.
*''[[NSW City Origin]] Debut:'' 2009 – NSW City Origin v NSW Country Origin, Wade Park, [[Orange, New South Wales]], 8 May.
*''NSW City Origin Selection:'' 2009–2011
*''[[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] Debut:'' [[2011 State of Origin series]] Game 3, New South Wales v Queensland, [[Suncorp Stadium]], [[Brisbane]], 6 July.
*''[[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] Test Debut:'' 2011 – Australia v New Zealand, [[Newcastle International Sports Centre]], [[Newcastle, New South Wales|Newcastle]], 16 October.
*''Australia Test Squad Selection:'' 2011
*'' Super League Champion 2017''

== Post playing ==
In 2018, Galloway was made a life member of the Wests Tigers.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2020-09-30 |title=Wests Tigers induct two new Life Members |url=https://www.weststigers.com.au/news/2020/09/30/wests-tigers-induct-two-new-life-members/ |access-date=2024-12-30 |website=Wests Tigers |language=en}}</ref> In 2023, Galloway made a cameo appearance in ''Lickerish.''<ref>{{Cite web |date=2023-07-31 |title=Tigers cult hero makes surprising career switch |url=https://www.nine.com.au/sport/nrl/news-2023-the-mole-ex-wests-tigers-keith-galloway-to-debut-in-movie-20230801-p5j6cb.html |access-date=2024-12-30 |website=Nine |language=en}}</ref>
